''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}}|Fest des Hervorkommens der [[Sopdet|Sothis]] |- |colspan="2" {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight 2}}|W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}}|Sothis und Wepet-renpet |- |colspan="2" {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight 2}} |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sothis die Große,
Herrin von Wepet renpet."{{Ankerlink|Parker 1950|o|d}}, Seite 33, § 168 |} Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''Sothis-Fest''' ist das Fest zum Wepet-renpet-Ereignis, also dem [[heliakisch]]en Aufgang des Sothis. Dieses Ereignis bestimmte den Jahresbeginn im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]]. Das Fest ist im [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] belegt. Nicht verwechselt werden darf es mit dem [[Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)|Neujahrsfest]] im [[Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|Verwaltungskalender]]. == Hintergrund == [[Datei:Sopdet.svg|mini|hochkant|Die Göttin Sothis (Sopdet)]] Da das Jahr im Mondkalender stets mit dem Sonnenaufgang nach Neumond begann, fand Wepet-renpet in der Regel einige Tage vor dem Jahresbeginn statt. Das war im Monat [[Wepet-renpet]], dem 12. Monat im Mondkalender, je nach Theorie ggf. aber alternativ in einem darauffolgenden interkalaren Monat. Einer Vermutung in {{Ankerlink|Parker 1950|o|d}} zu Folge wurde jeweils genau dann ein interkalarer 13. Monat in das Sothisjahr eingeschaltet, wenn der Folgemonat weniger als 12 Tage nach dem Wepet-renpet-Ereignis begann.{{Ankerlink|Parker 1950|o|d}}, Seite 31, § 151 Dieser Annahme folgend hätte das Wepet-renpet-Ereignis stets im gleichnamigen Monat stattgefunden und das neue Jahr hätte unter Umständen mehr als einen Monat nach dem Sothisfest begonnen. Die Alternative wäre, dass ein Schaltmonat immer dann berücksichtigt wurde, wenn das Wepet-renpet-Ereignis aus dem gleichnamigen Monat herausfiel. In dem Fall konnte dann im Gegenzug das Sothisfest mit Neujahr zusammen fallen. {{Ankerlink|Schott 1950|o|d|Altägyptische Festdaten}} zu Folge wurden die Feste, die nur ein mal im Jahr gefeiert wurden, schließlich im Verwaltungskalender fixiert. So wurde auch das Sothisfest mit dem Neujahrsfest „Wepet-renpet“ am Beginn des gleichnamigen ersten Monats im Veraltungskalender vereinigt und wanderte im Folgenden vom Aufgang des Sirius fort, rückwärts durch die Jahreszeiten.{{Ankerlink|Schott 1950|o|d}}, Seite (46) {{Belege fehlen|Belegangaben fehlen vollständig.|Nie nachfolgenden Abschnitte|Plural=1}} == Mythologie == Im Alten Reich zählte Sothis in den [[Pyramidentexte]]n (PT) zu einer Erscheinungsform der Göttin [[Isis]] oder als [[Ba (ägyptische Mythologie)|Ba]], der dem [[Sternbild]] [[Sah (Sternbild)|Sah]] folgt. In PT 883 wird der verstorbene und verjüngte König von [[Osiris]] und Sothis in ihrer Rolle als „Jahr“ gekrönt. Am Glanz der Sothis sollte zu erkennen sein, ob ein gutes oder schlechtes Jahr folgen wird. == Festverlauf == Der Ablauf des Sothis-Festes kann aufgrund fast vollständig fehlender direkter Belege nicht rekonstruiert werden. Aus dem Mittleren und Neuen Reich sind nur drei Einträge in den Festlisten erhalten geblieben. Der Festcharakter glich zumindest seit dem Mittleren Reich dem einer [[Nekropole]]nfeier, da während der Feierlichkeiten die Besucher von Gräbern um eine [[Opfer (Religion)|Opfergabe]] gebeten wurden. Aus dem Bruchstück einer [[Manuskript|Handschrift]] ist zu entnehmen, dass im siebten Regierungsjahr von [[Sesostris III.]] das Sothis-Fest am [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|17. Peret IV]] begangen wurde. Für den Festbedarf ist die Lieferung von „200 verschiedenen Broten und 60 Krügen Bier“ vermerkt. == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Ankerlink|Parker 1950|d|o}} - {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * {{Ankerlink|Schott 1950|d|o}} - [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. == Einzelnachweise == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet I]] [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

Mainz u. a. 1950, S. (46). {{Belege fehlen|Belegangaben fehlen vollständig.|Nie nachfolgenden Abschnitte|Plural=1}} == Mythologie == Im Alten Reich zählte Sothis in den [[Pyramidentexte]]n (PT) zu einer Erscheinungsform der Göttin [[Isis]] oder als [[Ba (ägyptische Mythologie)|Ba]], der dem [[Sternbild]] [[Sah (Sternbild)|Sah]] folgt. In PT 883 wird der verstorbene und verjüngte König von [[Osiris]] und Sothis in ihrer Rolle als „Jahr“ gekrönt. Am Glanz der Sothis sollte zu erkennen sein, ob ein gutes oder schlechtes Jahr folgen wird. == Festverlauf == Der Ablauf des Sothis-Festes kann aufgrund fast vollständig fehlender direkter Belege nicht rekonstruiert werden. Aus dem Mittleren und Neuen Reich sind nur drei Einträge in den Festlisten erhalten geblieben. Der Festcharakter glich zumindest seit dem Mittleren Reich dem einer [[Nekropole]]nfeier, da während der Feierlichkeiten die Besucher von Gräbern um eine [[Opfer (Religion)|Opfergabe]] gebeten wurden. Aus dem Bruchstück einer [[Manuskript|Handschrift]] ist zu entnehmen, dass im siebten Regierungsjahr von [[Sesostris III.]] das Sothis-Fest am [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|17. Peret IV]] begangen wurde. Für den Festbedarf ist die Lieferung von „200 verschiedenen Broten und 60 Krügen Bier“ vermerkt. == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. == Einzelnachweise == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet I]] [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

{| !colspan="2" |Öffner des Jahres |- | class="hiero" |F14auchF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Fest des Hervorkommens der [[Sopdet|Sothis]] |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sothis und Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sothis die Große,
Herrin von Wepet renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, S. 33, § 168. |}
Das [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptische]] '''Sothis-Fest''' ist das Fest zum Wepet-renpet-Ereignis, also dem [[heliakisch]]en Aufgang des Sothis. Dieses Ereignis bestimmte den Jahresbeginn im [[Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|Mondkalender]]. Das Fest ist im [[Altes Reich (Ägypten)|Alten Reich]] belegt. Nicht verwechselt werden darf es mit dem [[Neujahrsfest (Altes Ägypten)|Neujahrsfest]] im [[Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|Verwaltungskalender]]. == Hintergrund == [[Datei:Sopdet.svg|mini|hochkant|Die Göttin Sothis (Sopdet)]] Da das Jahr im Mondkalender stets mit dem Sonnenaufgang nach Neumond begann, fand Wepet-renpet in der Regel einige Tage vor dem Jahresbeginn statt. Das war im Monat [[Wepet-renpet]], dem 12. Monat im Mondkalender, je nach Theorie ggf. aber alternativ in einem darauffolgenden interkalaren Monat. Einer Vermutung in {{Ankerlink|Parker 1950|o|d|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt}} zu Folge wurde jeweils genau dann ein interkalarer 13. Monat in das Sothisjahr eingeschaltet, wenn der Folgemonat weniger als 12 Tage nach dem Wepet-renpet-Ereignis begann.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, S 31, § 151. Dieser Annahme folgend hätte das Wepet-renpet-Ereignis stets im gleichnamigen Monat stattgefunden und das neue Jahr hätte unter Umständen mehr als einen Monat nach dem Sothisfest begonnen. Die Alternative wäre, dass ein Schaltmonat immer dann berücksichtigt wurde, wenn das Wepet-renpet-Ereignis aus dem gleichnamigen Monat herausfiel. In dem Fall konnte dann im Gegenzug das Sothisfest mit Neujahr zusammen fallen. {{Ankerlink|Schott 1950|o|d|Altägyptische Festdaten}} zu Folge wurden die Feste, die nur ein mal im Jahr gefeiert wurden, schließlich im Verwaltungskalender fixiert. So wurde auch das Sothisfest mit dem Neujahrsfest „Wepet-renpet“ am Beginn des gleichnamigen ersten Monats im Verwaltungskalender vereinigt und wanderte im Folgenden vom Aufgang des Sirius fort, rückwärts durch die Jahreszeiten.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz u. a. 1950, S. (46). {{Belege fehlen|Belegangaben fehlen vollständig.|Nie nachfolgenden Abschnitte|Plural=1}} == Mythologie == Im Alten Reich zählte Sothis in den [[Pyramidentexte]]n (PT) zu einer Erscheinungsform der Göttin [[Isis]] oder als [[Ba (ägyptische Mythologie)|Ba]], der dem [[Sternbild]] [[Sah (Sternbild)|Sah]] folgt. In PT 883 wird der verstorbene und verjüngte König von [[Osiris]] und Sothis in ihrer Rolle als „Jahr“ gekrönt. Am Glanz der Sothis sollte zu erkennen sein, ob ein gutes oder schlechtes Jahr folgen wird. == Festverlauf == Der Ablauf des Sothis-Festes kann aufgrund fast vollständig fehlender direkter Belege nicht rekonstruiert werden. Aus dem Mittleren und Neuen Reich sind nur drei Einträge in den Festlisten erhalten geblieben. Der Festcharakter glich zumindest seit dem Mittleren Reich dem einer [[Nekropole]]nfeier, da während der Feierlichkeiten die Besucher von Gräbern um eine [[Opfer (Religion)|Opfergabe]] gebeten wurden. Aus dem Bruchstück einer [[Manuskript|Handschrift]] ist zu entnehmen, dass im siebten Regierungsjahr von [[Sesostris III.]] das Sothis-Fest am [[Peret (Altes Ägypten)#Datierungen|17. Peret IV]] begangen wurde. Für den Festbedarf ist die Lieferung von „200 verschiedenen Broten und 60 Krügen Bier“ vermerkt. == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer ägyptologische Beiträge. (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{Falsche ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse. (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur u. a., Mainz u. a. 1950. == Einzelnachweise == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Kategorie:Festkalender des Monats Achet I]] [[Kategorie:Religiöses Fest (Altes Ägypten)]]

''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). {| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:new pages]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:new pages]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[de:Sothis-Fest]]

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[de:Sothis-Fest]]

This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). ''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). {| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,

The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Altägyptische Festdaten|de}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq-Sothis-Festival-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]
