Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]]

According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}}

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Qq-1月4日 (旧暦)-qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}}

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:kk]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}}

The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}}

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[en:draft:Sothis-Festival]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:othis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis-Festival}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis-Festival]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|12=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}}

The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|12=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}} {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender}} [[Category:Navigationsleiste Ägyptischer Kalender]]

According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|12=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}} [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}} [[Category:ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet|12=draft}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet|12=draft}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|12=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[sn:Sothis Festival]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sothis Festival]][[Category:qq]] {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}}

{| !colspan="2" |{{ill|Opener of the Year|de|Wepet-renpet|12=draft|12=draft}} |- | class="hiero" |F14alsoF15 |[[Wepet-renpet]]
''Wpt-rnp.t'' |- !colspan="2" |Festival of the Rising of {{ill|Sopdet|de|Sopdet|12=draft|12=draft}} |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |W3-O1-D21:X1-D54-M44-X1:N14 |- |colspan="2" |Heb-peret-Sopdet
''Ḥb-prt-Spdt'' |- !colspan="2" |Sopdet and Wepet-renpet |- | class="hiero" colspan="2" |R8-M44-N14:X1*H8-Z9*X1*H8:V30-F13-M4 |- |colspan="2" |"Sopdet the Great,
Lady of Wepet-renpet."R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 33, § 168. |}
The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|12=draft|12=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{draft categories| }} {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}}

The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|12=draft|12=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}}

Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}} {{translated page|de|Sothis-Fest|version=231221368|comment=Imported full de history}}

The [[Ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian]] '''Sothis Festival''' celebrated the Wepet-renpet event, the [[heliacal rising]] of Sothis. This event determined the start of the year in the [[Lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|lunar calendar]]. The festival is attested in the [[Old Kingdom of Egypt|Old Kingdom]] but should not be confused with the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year festival]] in the [[Egyptian civil calendar|administrative calendar]]. == Background == [[File:Sopdet.svg|thumb|upright|The goddess Sopdet]] Since the lunar calendar year always began with the sunrise after the new moon, Wepet-renpet usually occurred a few days before the year's start. This typically fell in the month of Wepet-renpet, the 12th month of the lunar calendar, though occasionally in an intercalary month depending on the theory. According to a hypothesis in {{ill|The Calendars of Ancient Egypt|en}}, an intercalary 13th month was inserted into the Sothic year whenever the following month began fewer than 12 days after the Wepet-renpet event.R. A. Parker: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 31, § 151. This would mean that the Wepet-renpet event always took place in its eponymous month, and the new year could start more than a month after the Sothis Festival. Alternatively, an intercalary month could be added whenever the Wepet-renpet event fell outside its designated month, potentially aligning the Sothis Festival with the new year. According to {{ill|Ancient Egyptian festival dates|de|Altägyptische Festdaten|qq=draft}}, annual festivals were eventually fixed in the administrative calendar, and the Sothis Festival was combined with the New Year festival "Wepet-renpet," beginning the eponymous first month of the administrative calendar. Over time, it moved away from the rising of Sirius, retrograding through the seasons.S. Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Mainz et al., 1950, p. (46). == Mythology == In the Old Kingdom, Sothis appeared in the [[Pyramid Texts]] (PT) as a form of the goddess [[Isis]] or as the [[Ba (Egyptian soul)|Ba]] accompanying the [[Sah (constellation)|Sah]] constellation. In PT 883, the deceased and rejuvenated king is crowned by [[Osiris]] and Sothis in her role as the "Year." The brightness of Sothis was seen as an omen for a good or bad year ahead. == Festival Proceedings == Due to a lack of direct evidence, the exact proceedings of the Sothis Festival cannot be reconstructed. Only three entries in festival lists from the Middle and New Kingdoms have survived. By the Middle Kingdom, the festival resembled a necropolis celebration, with grave visitors solicited for [[Offering (religion)|offerings]]. A fragment of a [[Manuscript|manuscript]] notes that in the seventh year of [[Sesostris III]], the Sothis Festival was held on [[Peret (Ancient Egypt)#Dating|17 Peret IV]]. Supplies for the festival included "200 different breads and 60 jars of beer." == Literature == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Sothis- und Monddaten. Studien zur astronomischen und technischen Chronologie Altägyptens'' (= ''Hildesheimer Ägyptologische Beiträge (HÄB).'' Band 20). Gerstenberg, Hildesheim 1985, {{ISBN|3-8067-8086-X}}. * {{Literatur|Titel=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |Autor=Richard Anthony Parker |Sammelwerk=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |Band=26 |Verlag=The University of Chicago Press |Ort=Chicago (Illinois) |Datum=1950 |Sprache=en }} * [[Siegfried Schott]]: ''Altägyptische Festdaten'' (= ''Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur. Abhandlungen der Geistes- und Sozialwissenschaftlichen Klasse (AM-GS).'' 1950, Band 10, {{ISSN|0002-2977}}). Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz et al., 1950. == References == {{Egyptian Calendar Navbar}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Festivals in the month Achet I]] [[Category:Religious festivals in ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian festivals]] }} [[en:draft:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:Sothis Festival]] [[sn:othis-Festival]] [[de:Sothis-Fest]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Sothis Festival}} [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Sothis-Fest|version=231221368|comment=Imported full de history}}
